frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Maa ke haath ki simple dal-roti mein jo sukoon tha na, woh aajkal kisi five-star food mein bhi nahi milta. Puri zindagi bina thake ghar ko sambhala, garam khana diya, kabhi hisaab nahi rakha — wahi toh asli aurat ka pyaar hota hai. Aaj ki bahuon ko bhi thoda wohi dil se ghar basana seekhna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes a mother’s unpaid domestic labor as the ideal form of womanhood and love, rather than recognizing it as work. It also sets an expectation for daughters-in-law to emulate that self-sacrificing standard, encoding comparison and labor erasure.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
